misconceptionSummary: Students carry the wrong digit — they carry the units digit instead of the tens digit.

lines.text: The carry is the tens digit of 42

steps.narration: Seven times six is forty-two. Write the units digit — two — in the ones column. Carry the tens digit — four — into the tens column. The carry is always the tens digit of the product.

Tens: four times six is twenty-four, plus the carried four is twenty-eight. Write eight, carry two. Hundreds: three times six is eighteen, plus the carried two is twenty. Write twenty in the hundreds and thousands columns.

visuals.ruleText: Write the carry digit small above the next column — add it after multiplying that column's digit
visuals.subText: Carry = tens digit of the product; write digit = units digit of the product

steps.narration: Always write the carry digit above the next column before you forget it. Then multiply the next digit, add the carry, and decide whether to carry again.

visuals.label: Write units digit, carry tens digit — add carry to next product

steps.narration: Two thousand and eighty-two. Write the units, carry the tens, add the carry to the next product — the same rule at every column.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: carrying the units digit instead of the tens digit. 7 × 6 = 42 — write 2, carry 4 (the tens). Carrying 2 and writing 4 reverses them and gives a wrong answer.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The carry is the tens digit of the product, not the units. Write the units digit in the current column and carry the tens digit to the next.
